heading: Compensation of Library of Congress employees
label: 162 Compensation of Library of Congress employees
text: Employees of the Library of Congress who perform special functions for the performance of which funds have been entrusted to the board or the librarian, or in connection with cooperative undertakings in which the Library of Congress is engaged, shall not be subject to ; and shall not apply to any additional compensation so paid to such employees.section 209 of title 18section 5533 of title 5
notes: Codification“” substituted in text for reference to the Act of , (), on authority of (1) , , section 1 of which enacted Title 18, Crimes and Criminal Procedure, and which enacted in the provisions formerly classified to ; and (2) , , , which repealed and supplanted it with section 209, and which provided that exemptions from section 1914 shall be deemed exemptions from section 209. For further details, see Exemptions note set out under .Section 209 of title 1839 Stat. 11065 U.S.C. 66act June 25, 1948, ch. 64562 Stat. 683section 1914 of Title 18section 66 of Title 5section 2 of Pub. L. 89–55480 Stat. 631Sept. 6, 1966Section was formerly classified to sections 60 and 65 of Title 5 prior to the general revision and enactment of Title 5, Government Organization and Employees, by , , .Pub. L. 89–55480 Stat. 378Sept. 6, 1966

Amendments1964— substituted “and section 301 of the Dual Compensation Act [] shall not apply to any additional compensation so paid to such employees” for “nor shall any additional compensation so paid to such employees be construed as a double salary under the provisions of section 6 of the Act making appropriations for the legislative, executive, and judicial expenses of the Government for the fiscal year ending , as amended (Thirty-ninth Statutes at Large, page 582) []”.Pub.
